results.notes: Perfect match. WeeklySelectionResult->RecipeOverlap field-type edge correctly identified. classDiagram rule applied correctly.

results.root_cause: Skill agent treated get_pending_queries() as a state transition Scheduled->Pending, inserting an extra intermediate sub-state 'Pending'. get_pending_queries() is a SQL filter method (WHERE executed=FALSE AND scheduled_time<=now), not a state transition. Only database column states (executed=False=Scheduled, executed=True=Executed) are true states. Violates SKILL.md rule: do not insert intermediate sub-states not derivable from class body.
results.proposed_fix: Add rule to stateDiagram-v2 section: 'Query/filter methods (get_pending_*, get_active_*, etc.) represent SQL WHERE filters on existing states — they are NOT state transitions and do NOT introduce new states. Only methods that UPDATE the status field create state transitions.'

results.notes: Perfect match. Method-level GT calibration held. Graph's calls edges recovered all 5 internal call chains exactly.

All 5 actors recovered. Missed: sync_hierarchy_from_server() call — the method call on an already-instantiated object (OverlapSyncManager instance) not captured as a separate cross-file edge in graph. Known graph limitation for chained method calls on constructed objects.
Perfect match on zero-edge diagram. Correctly identified no structural field-type relationships (crypto objects are method-local, not stored as fields).

failures.description: get_pending_queries() filter method misread as state transition, adding intermediate Pending state

new_findings.stateDiagram_v2_filter_method_gap: Query/filter methods (get_pending_*, get_active_*) can be mistaken for state-creating transitions. Need explicit rule: only UPDATE/DELETE methods create state transitions; SELECT/filter methods do not.
new_findings.sequenceDiagram_chained_method_gap: When main() instantiates an object then calls methods on it, the method calls may not appear as separate cross-file graph edges — only the constructor call is captured. This causes a systematic miss for chained method calls post-construction.
new_findings.zero_edge_classDiagram: Zero-edge diagrams (single class, no custom field types) work correctly — skill correctly avoids hallucinating structural edges.
